description: Evaluates a candidate's medical appointment booking bot built on Kore.ai XO Platform. Tests appointment booking (happy path and amendment), retrieval, rescheduling, cancellation, and FAQ capabilities. Uses agentic evaluation mode — an LLM-as-user conducts conversations dynamically and goals are assessed post-conversation.

llm_as_user.description: Agentic evaluation mode requires an LLM runtime to conduct conversations dynamically per turn.
llm_as_user.fallback: If LLM-as-user is unavailable, evaluation degrades to state-assertion-only mode.

on_restart_event.penalty_label: Task required a restart during evaluation run

required_nodes.label: Welcome greeting message displayed to user

tasks.candidate_instructions: Configure a Welcome Chat Flow that triggers when a user starts a conversation. Display a greeting message to the user. Present the available services using a List Entity. Based on the user's selection, automatically trigger the appropriate dialog task. The menu must cover: booking an appointment, fetching appointment details, rescheduling, and FAQs.

sub_scores.label: Greeting message is displayed to the user
List Entity correctly triggers the appropriate dialog task on selection

required_entities.validation_description: Must contain both first name and last name

required_entities.semantic_hint: contact phone number including country code
required_entities.validation_description: Must include country code followed by 10 digits (e.g. +91XXXXXXXXXX)

required_entities.semantic_hint: type or specialty of doctor required (e.g. Cardiologist, Dentist, General Practitioner)
required_entities.validation_description: Must be a valid medical specialty

required_entities.semantic_hint: preferred date of appointment in DD-MM-YYYY format
required_entities.validation_description: Must follow DD-MM-YYYY format and be a future date

required_entities.semantic_hint: preferred time for the appointment (e.g. 10:00 AM, 2:30 PM)
required_entities.validation_description: Must be within clinic hours (8:00 AM to 6:00 PM)

required_entities.semantic_hint: reason or purpose of the medical visit
required_entities.validation_description: Brief description of the medical concern

agent_node_rules: patientName must contain both a first name and last name — re-prompt if only one name is provided
contactNumber must include a country code followed by exactly 10 digits (e.g. +91XXXXXXXXXX)
preferredDate must follow DD-MM-YYYY format and be a future date
preferredTime must be within clinic hours (8:00 AM to 6:00 PM)
Agent Node bookmark must clearly explain the node's purpose for other developers on the project

required_nodes.label: Agent Node for entity collection with validation rules
required_nodes.bookmark_hint: Captures all 6 appointment entities using LLM. Supports co-referencing (multiple entities in one message) and amendment (user corrects a value mid-conversation). Enforces: full patient name, contact number with country code, DD-MM-YYYY date format, and clinic hours validation.

required_nodes.label: POST service node — create appointment in backend
required_nodes.bookmark_hint: Sends all collected appointment data to the backend via POST API. Handles error response — if the API call fails, the agent displays a polite error message informing the user the appointment was not booked.

required_nodes.label: Appointment summary prompt — display captured details and ask user to confirm before proceeding
Appointment confirmation message — inform user booking is confirmed

conversation_evaluation.evaluator_persona: patient seeking to book a medical appointment

goal_checks.label: All 6 entities collected and present in backend state
Confirmation prompt fires before POST API call
goal_checks.pattern_a: confirm|is this correct|please confirm|correct\?|details are|shall i proceed|want to proceed

goal_checks.label: Appointment record confirmed in backend after POST

tasks.candidate_instructions: Design a dialog task that captures all 6 appointment details from the patient. Use an Agent Node to collect entities — do not use separate entity nodes for this task. Add a bookmark to the Agent Node explaining its purpose. Configure the following validation rules within the Agent Node: (1) full name required, (2) contact number must include country code + 10 digits, (3) preferred date must be in DD-MM-YYYY format and be a future date, (4) preferred time must be within clinic hours (8:00 AM to 6:00 PM). Display a summary of the collected details using a Prompt Node and ask the user to confirm before submitting. Send the appointment via a POST API request. If the API fails, display a clear and polite error message. Add a bookmark to the Service Node explaining its purpose.

sub_scores.label: Agent Node present with required bookmark explaining its purpose
All 4 validation rules present in Agent Node instructions (name, contact, date format, clinic hours)
Confirmation prompt fires before POST API call — verified via transcript ordering
POST API called successfully and appointment confirmed in backend state

required_entities.semantic_hint: contact phone number including country code
preferred date of appointment in DD-MM-YYYY format
reason or purpose of the medical visit

required_nodes.label: Agent Node — must support mid-conversation entity amendment
required_nodes.bookmark_hint: Handles entity amendment — allows patients to correct previously provided information mid-conversation before the appointment is confirmed.

amendment_config.amendment_utterance_template: Actually, change my contact number to {amended_value}

tasks.candidate_instructions: This task tests the Agent Node's ability to handle entity amendment mid-conversation. The evaluator will provide all 6 entities, then correct the contact number after the summary is shown but before confirmation. Ensure the Agent Node supports this correction and that the amended value is saved correctly when the appointment is submitted.

sub_scores.label: Agent Node handles mid-conversation amendment to contactNumber correctly
Amended contact number (not original) is saved in backend state

required_entities.semantic_hint: registered mobile number for appointment lookup

required_nodes.label: Confirm mobile number with user before making GET call
GET service node — retrieve appointment details

confirmation_check.expected_bot_pattern: confirm|correct|is this your number|please confirm|right number|is that correct
confirmation_check.description: The GET API call must appear in the API log only after the user has confirmed their mobile number.

tasks.candidate_instructions: Ask the patient for their mobile number. Capture it using an entity node. Before calling the API, ask the patient to confirm the entered number — the GET request must only be triggered after the patient confirms. Retrieve and display the appointment details. If no appointment is found, display an appropriate message.

sub_scores.label: Confirmation prompt fires before GET API call — verified via transcript ordering
GET API returns correct appointment data for the provided contact number

negative_tests.expected_error_pattern: not found|no appointment|invalid|does not exist|no record

tasks.candidate_instructions: If no appointment is found for the provided mobile number, display an appropriate error message informing the patient no appointment exists. Allow the patient to re-enter a different mobile number and try again.

sub_scores.label: Clear error message displayed when no appointment found for invalid contact number
Patient is offered the opportunity to re-enter their mobile number

required_entities.semantic_hint: registered mobile number for appointment lookup
new preferred date for the appointment in DD-MM-YYYY format
new preferred time for the appointment

required_nodes.label: GET service node — retrieve existing appointment before rescheduling
PUT service node — update appointment date and time in backend

tasks.candidate_instructions: Ask the patient for their mobile number. Retrieve the existing appointment using a GET API and display the details. Ask for a new preferred date and time. Send a PUT API request to update the appointment. Important: when configuring date validation, use format-only rules (DD-MM-YYYY) — do not hardcode specific future dates.

sub_scores.label: GET API fires before rescheduling — existing appointment retrieved and displayed
New preferred date and time captured from patient input
PUT API called and updated date/time confirmed in backend state

required_entities.semantic_hint: registered mobile number for appointment lookup

required_nodes.label: DELETE service node — remove appointment from backend

tasks.candidate_instructions: Ask the patient for their mobile number. Retrieve the existing appointment using a GET API. Ask the patient to confirm cancellation. Send a DELETE API request to remove the appointment. After deletion, confirm to the patient that their appointment has been successfully cancelled.

sub_scores.label: DELETE API called using Appointment2 contact number
Appointment2 is no longer retrievable in backend state after deletion

required_faqs.primary_question: How do I prepare for my doctor's appointment?
required_faqs.ground_truth_answer: Before your appointment, gather any relevant medical records, write down your symptoms and questions for the doctor, bring a list of any current medications, and arrive 10 minutes early to complete any required paperwork.
alternate_questions: What should I bring to my doctor's appointment?
How should I get ready before seeing the doctor?

required_faqs.primary_question: How will I know my appointment is confirmed?
required_faqs.ground_truth_answer: Once your appointment is confirmed, the system will display your booking reference. You can verify your appointment at any time by providing your registered contact number to the medical bot.
alternate_questions: How do I get confirmation of my appointment?
What happens after I book an appointment?

required_faqs.ground_truth_answer: Yes, you can reschedule your appointment by selecting 'Reschedule Appointment' and providing your registered contact number. You will then be asked for a new preferred date and time.
alternate_questions: How do I change my appointment time?
Is it possible to move my appointment to a different date?

required_faqs.primary_question: What types of doctors can I book through this system?
required_faqs.ground_truth_answer: You can book appointments with a range of medical specialists including General Practitioners, Cardiologists, Dentists, Neurologists, Dermatologists, and Orthopaedic specialists, depending on availability.
alternate_questions: Which specialties are available for booking?
Can I book an appointment with a specialist?

required_faqs.primary_question: What is the cancellation policy for appointments?
required_faqs.ground_truth_answer: You can cancel your appointment at any time before the scheduled visit. To cancel, select 'Cancel Appointment' and provide your registered contact number. Please note that repeated cancellations may affect future booking priority.
alternate_questions: How do I cancel my appointment?
Is there a fee for cancelling a medical appointment?

functional_verification.note: faq1 (preparation guidance) and faq5 (cancellation policy) selected for live verification.

compliance_checks.label: DialogGPT is enabled
compliance_checks.tooltip: DialogGPT must be enabled for the Agent Node to function correctly. This is a binary check — either the flag is set or it is not. This sub-score is not open to grievance challenge.

state_seeding_config.note: Pre-test seed and post-test teardown endpoints must be configured to prevent state bleed between evaluation runs.

tooltips.text: Agent Node — uses LLM to collect entities conversationally. Supports co-referencing (multiple entities in one message) and amendment (correcting a value mid-conversation). Validation rules and appointment instructions should be configured here.
Service Node — makes API calls to external systems (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE). Add a bookmark explaining the purpose of each Service Node.
Entity Node — collects a specific piece of information from the patient. For menu navigation, use a List Entity type.
Message Node — displays information to the patient. Used for confirmations, summaries, and error messages.
Confirmation Node — asks the patient to confirm a value or action before proceeding.
Prompt Node — displays captured details and asks the patient to confirm before the dialog proceeds to an API call.

notes: Medical Appointment Bot basic assessment — v1.0. Domain-agnostic design: same six engine patterns as Travel, different entities and FAQs. Entities: patientName, contactNumber, doctorType, preferredDate, preferredTime, reasonForVisit. Amendment target: contactNumber. Retrieve/Modify/Delete all use contactNumber as the cross-task identifier.
